Details: GAC is not a best available technology (BAT) for vinyl chloride. U.S. EPA (1998) identified GAC as an SSCT for vinyl chloride but did not consider affordability in the evaluation. In 2013, EPA conducted rapid small-scale column tests of GAC to treat groundwater spiked with various volatile organic contaminants. In these tests, the GAC columns achieved 90 percent removal of vinyl chloride, but maintained this performance for only 2,610 bed volumes. Vinyl chloride in effluent exceeded average influent at 5,860 bed volumes [2808]. This result suggests that, although it may be feasible to remove vinyl chloride to low levels using GAC, the achievable bed life is unlikely to be cost-effective at full scale.
